persona_name: Victoria and James, the Luxury Couple

bugs.title: fullContent only captures last tool-loop segment
bugs.description: When a SendMessage response spans multiple tool-loop iterations (text -> tool_call -> tool_result -> text), the messageComplete.fullContent field only contains the text from the final segment, not the complete response. In the Dubai fine dining message (msg 3), the AI produced a detailed multi-paragraph response about Tresind Studio, Zuma, Ossiano, STAY, Dinner by Heston, Hoseki, and Orfali Bros, then called create_itinerary_items, then produced a short follow-up. fullContent only captured the short follow-up: 'It seems there was a slight misunderstanding! ...' (81 chars) instead of the full ~3000 char response.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: Send a planning message that triggers both a long text response AND a tool call (e.g., ask about restaurants + have AI add itinerary items). Check messageComplete.fullContent in the stream output.
bugs.expected: fullContent should contain the complete concatenated text from all segments of the response across tool-loop iterations
bugs.actual: fullContent only contains the text from the last segment after the final tool result

ux_issues.description: When the AI creates a large itinerary (30 items), it sometimes makes a duplicate tool call with the same items, which returns 'already_exists'. The third create_itinerary_items call in msg 2 sent all 30 items again and got back 0 created / 30 skipped. This wastes tokens and could confuse the AI.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider having the AI check tool results before re-calling with identical items, or have the backend return a clearer signal that prevents the AI from retrying.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: No expert persona handoff triggered during the entire session. The conversation about Dubai fine dining and Maldives luxury resorts should have triggered suggest_expert for food/luxury themes, but Toqui handled everything directly without handing off to a composed expert.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Multiple messages about Maldives resort selection and Dubai fine dining — both strong candidates for food/luxury expert handoff.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Booking.com suggested for ultra-luxury Maldives resort booking. The AI recommended booking the St. Regis Maldives Vommuli via Booking.com, which is tone-deaf for this tier of property. Ultra-luxury resorts like St. Regis and Soneva are typically best booked directly, through the hotel's own website, or via a luxury travel advisor for preferred rates and upgrade eligibility.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Victoria explicitly stated money is not a concern and she stays at Aman/Four Seasons regularly. The recommend_booking tool defaulted to Booking.com.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated excellent luxury calibration throughout the session. Resort recommendations (St. Regis, Waldorf Astoria, Cheval Blanc, Soneva Jani) were all genuinely top-tier with specific differentiators. The 30-item itinerary had perfect luxury pacing — no cramming, plenty of leisure time, romantic elements woven in without being asked. Dubai dining knowledge was impressive (Tresind Studio reservation difficulty, Hoseki's 9 seats, Orfali Bros as a newer standout). Companion mode was appropriately concise and practical. The main gaps were: no expert persona handoffs (missed opportunity for food/luxury specialists), fullContent bug loses response text after tool loops, and the Booking.com recommendation for an ultra-luxury resort was a calibration miss for this persona tier.
